meta.title: Public AI impact source corpus for STEM entry-risk matrix
meta.purpose: Record public reports, policies, industry projects, professional surveys and startup signals used to calibrate the public-facing STEM AI job-risk matrix.
meta.interpretation: Sources are mapped to entry-level tasks and decision signals. They are not treated as direct predictions of unemployment or major disappearance.

sources.title: Labor market impacts of AI: A new measure and early evidence
sources.taskSignal: Observed exposure shows high coverage for programmers, software QA, data scientists, operations research analysts and other digital task occupations.
sources.matrixImplication: Use observed AI task coverage as the main anchor for red/orange cells involving code, testing, BI, modeling, reports and document work.

sources.title: Working with AI: Measuring the Occupational Implications of Generative AI
sources.taskSignal: Real Copilot conversations concentrate on information gathering, writing, teaching, advising and other text-heavy tasks; this indicates AI assistance entering workflows, not direct job replacement.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports higher risk for writing, reporting, documentation, analysis and low-context advisory entry tasks.

sources.title: Canaries in the Coal Mine? Six Facts about the Recent Employment Effects of Artificial Intelligence
sources.taskSignal: Early-career workers in highly AI-exposed occupations show early employment pressure relative to older workers.
sources.matrixImplication: Strengthens the page's focus on beginner tasks rather than senior professional survival.

sources.title: Task-Completion Time Horizons of Frontier AI Models
sources.taskSignal: Frontier agents are improving on longer digital tasks, while reliability and full responsibility remain bottlenecks.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports rising pressure on longer digital projects, but keeps hardware, field, clinical and safety chains as separate constraints.

sources.title: The Future of Jobs Report 2025
sources.taskSignal: AI, big data, cybersecurity, energy generation/storage/distribution and green transition roles are expected to grow, while skill disruption is broad.
sources.matrixImplication: Marks AI/data/energy as opportunity areas only when paired with high-threshold systems and real implementation.

sources.title: 2025 Stack Overflow Developer Survey: AI
sources.taskSignal: AI tool usage by professional developers is widespread, but trust and verification remain major concerns.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports the shift from routine implementation skill premiums toward review, reliability, testing and system ownership.

sources.title: State of AI-assisted Software Development
sources.taskSignal: AI amplifies existing engineering strengths and weaknesses rather than automatically improving every team.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports higher value for platform, delivery process, observability and feedback loops.

sources.title: The LeadDev AI Impact Report 2025
sources.taskSignal: Engineering leaders report uncertainty around junior developer hiring and routine coding tasks.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports the high-risk rating for generic business development and testing entry routes.

sources.title: 普通高等学校本科专业目录（2026年）
sources.taskSignal: Mainland undergraduate catalog adds an interdisciplinary category and new majors such as embodied intelligence and brain-computer science and technology.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports treating new engineering as policy-supported but high-variance rather than automatically safe.

sources.title: 国务院关于深入实施“人工智能+”行动的意见
sources.taskSignal: Policy pushes AI into science, industry, healthcare, governance and other scenarios, while emphasizing application landing and infrastructure.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports opportunity labels only where AI connects to real industrial, scientific, medical or public-service platforms.

sources.title: 人形机器人创新发展指导意见
sources.taskSignal: Policy targets humanoid robot innovation systems and reliable industrial chains.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports opportunity labels for robot body, force control and systems integration, but not for pure simulation demos.

sources.title: 关于推动脑机接口产业创新发展的实施意见
sources.taskSignal: BCI policy emphasizes technology, industry, standard systems, clinical and ethical constraints.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports opportunity status for BCI while keeping entry risk high unless tied to hardware, clinical validation and regulation.

sources.title: 智能制造典型场景参考指引（2024年版）
sources.taskSignal: Smart manufacturing scenarios emphasize equipment, industrial software, system integration and full lifecycle value.
sources.matrixImplication: Moves manufacturing-related opportunity from generic demos toward equipment, system integration and factory implementation.

sources.title: World Robotics 2025 Industrial Robots
sources.taskSignal: IFR reports 542,000 industrial robot installations globally in 2024, with China representing 54% of global deployments.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports real demand for automation while distinguishing real deployment from algorithm/demo oversupply.

sources.title: Synopsys DSO.ai and AI-powered EDA
sources.taskSignal: AI-driven design space optimization and EDA flows are moving into production chip design workflows.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports compression of scripting/flow tasks and opportunity for EDA/toolchain engineers.

sources.title: Cadence Cerebrus AI Studio
sources.taskSignal: Agentic AI is being positioned for chip design optimization and production design support.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports the IC split between routine flow automation and high-threshold architecture, verification and toolchain work.

sources.title: Artificial intelligence in construction report
sources.taskSignal: Built-environment professionals see AI potential in reports, defect recognition and valuation modeling, but adoption requires standards and responsibility.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports high exposure for reports and model outputs, while preserving field inspection and professional liability as constraints.

sources.title: Artificial Intelligence Adoption in Architecture Firms
sources.taskSignal: AI adoption in architecture is early but rising, with concern about health, safety and welfare responsibilities.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports risk for visualization/text entry but keeps professional responsibility and real project constraints central.

sources.title: Drafters: Occupational Outlook Handbook
sources.taskSignal: BLS projects little or no change in drafter employment 2024-2034, noting that CAD/BIM technologies continue to temper growth by enabling engineers and architects to perform tasks traditionally done by drafters.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports elevated risk for pure CAD/BIM drawing, modeling and drafting entry tasks, even as occupation-level employment stays flat.

sources.title: Autonomous Systems for Materials Research and Metrology
sources.taskSignal: Autonomous laboratories combine AI, robotics, metrology and materials characterization.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports opportunity for experimental platform integration rather than low-end computational screening alone.

sources.title: Isomorphic Labs Drug Design Engine
sources.taskSignal: AI drug design is advancing through pharma partnerships and computational drug design engines, but must bridge to real validation.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports opportunity for AI drug discovery only when connected to assay, ADMET, clinical and experimental validation.

sources.title: Ginkgo Datapoints and biofoundry AI data
sources.taskSignal: Biofoundry and automation platforms aim to generate AI-ready biological data at scale.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports opportunity for bio platform operations and data governance, not generic public-data bioinformatics.

sources.title: Half of Supply Chain Management Solutions Will Include Agentic AI Capabilities by 2030
sources.taskSignal: Agentic AI is expected to automate complex tasks across supply chain planning and execution.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports high risk for plans, RFQ, procurement, reports and forecast workflows, while emphasizing system and exception handling.

sources.title: Agentic AI in Logistics: A Strategic Imperative
sources.taskSignal: GenAI and agentic systems are being applied to logistics productivity, customer response and data-driven decisions.
sources.matrixImplication: Supports compression of logistics coordination and reporting, with resilience in real operations and exception handling.
